legacy_identity.notice: The four-level catalog, CAP IDs, JSON shape, and criteria are build-layer additions, not identifiers from the legacy corpus.

noncredential_notice: Developmental evidence only; not a grade, course credit, certification, licensure, clinical competence determination, employment authorization, delegation, privilege, or permission to practice.

criteria.description: In one adopted, evaluated, nonsynthetic mission, the learner explains model limits, false fluency, automation bias, verification needs, and when not to use AI; records their own contribution, AI contribution, source/data check, uncertainty, and next independent action.

Across two distinct eligible missions, the learner independently explains model limits, false fluency, automation bias, verification needs, and when not to use AI; resolves or transparently records one correction, conflict, limitation, or failed attempt; and receives a named-human process review.

Across three distinct eligible missions, the learner designs, evaluates, and improves a reusable governed method that explains model limits, false fluency, automation bias, verification needs, and when not to use AI; compares outcome, burden, accessibility, independence, and unintended effects; and documents safe reuse and stop conditions for human review.

In the approved orchestration capstone, the learner uses frozen P1–P2 agents to support a mission that explains model limits, false fluency, automation bias, verification needs, and when not to use AI; explains routing and limits, independently verifies outputs, demonstrates Stop or safe failure recovery, completes Evaluate, and receives a named-human process review.

In one adopted, evaluated, nonsynthetic mission, the learner uses Situation, Aim, Facts, and Expectations with minimum necessary nonsensitive context; records their own contribution, AI contribution, source/data check, uncertainty, and next independent action.

Across two distinct eligible missions, the learner independently uses Situation, Aim, Facts, and Expectations with minimum necessary nonsensitive context; resolves or transparently records one correction, conflict, limitation, or failed attempt; and receives a named-human process review.

Across three distinct eligible missions, the learner designs, evaluates, and improves a reusable governed method that uses Situation, Aim, Facts, and Expectations with minimum necessary nonsensitive context; compares outcome, burden, accessibility, independence, and unintended effects; and documents safe reuse and stop conditions for human review.

Across three distinct eligible missions, the learner designs, evaluates, and improves a reusable governed method that measures outcome, burden, accessibility, independence, unintended effects, corrections, and a continue, revise, pause, stop, escalate, or next-cycle decision; compares outcome, burden, accessibility, independence, and unintended effects; and documents safe reuse and stop conditions for human review.

In one adopted, evaluated, nonsynthetic mission, the learner builds truthful, reviewed evidence of student or assistant learning, communication, career growth, leadership, and safe technology stewardship without competence or credential claims; records their own contribution, AI contribution, source/data check, uncertainty, and next independent action.

Across two distinct eligible missions, the learner independently builds truthful, reviewed evidence of student or assistant learning, communication, career growth, leadership, and safe technology stewardship without competence or credential claims; resolves or transparently records one correction, conflict, limitation, or failed attempt; and receives a named-human process review.

Across three distinct eligible missions, the learner designs, evaluates, and improves a reusable governed method that builds truthful, reviewed evidence of student or assistant learning, communication, career growth, leadership, and safe technology stewardship without competence or credential claims; compares outcome, burden, accessibility, independence, and unintended effects; and documents safe reuse and stop conditions for human review.

In the approved orchestration capstone, the learner uses frozen P1–P2 agents to support a mission that builds truthful, reviewed evidence of student or assistant learning, communication, career growth, leadership, and safe technology stewardship without competence or credential claims; explains routing and limits, independently verifies outputs, demonstrates Stop or safe failure recovery, completes Evaluate, and receives a named-human process review.
One adopted, evaluated, nonsynthetic, low-risk mission has a learner-authored objective and outcome, burden, and independence measures.
At least two frozen agents, or one agent plus an independent reviewer, operate only at P1–P2 with visible routing rationale.
Context, data, tools, time, cost, token, retry, destination, expiry, human gates, stop, kill, and fallback limits are frozen before the run.
Agent outputs and handoffs remain distinct; an unauthorized handoff, recursion, permission widening, or background continuation is rejected.
The learner demonstrates a real cancellation or safe synthetic failure-and-recovery path without false completion.
Consequential claims and artifacts receive independent source and human review, with conflicts and unknowns retained.
Evaluate records results, burden, accessibility, independence, unintended effects, and a continue, revise, pause, stop, escalate, or new-cycle decision.
A complete activity and AI-use receipt states learner work, agent work, decisions, corrections, disclosures, data handling, and what the learner can now do independently.
An appropriate named human reviews process quality without asserting clinical competence, institutional approval, certification, licensure, or employment authority.
